text:
Diffusion magnetic resonance imaging (dMRI) has become a valuable diagnostic and investigative tool in both clinical and neurological research fields to study the human brain in vivo. Despite availability of a large number of post-processing utilities, a fully automated workflow has not been achieved yet. This paper proposes a fully automated workflow tool that can streamline the processing of diffusion brain data, called “Pipeline for Analyzing braiN Diffusion imAges” (PANDA). The proposed PANDA tool is a MATLAB toolbox implemented to fully automatically process brain diffusion data. The PANDA toolbox incorporates existing processing modules from some established software applications in the dMRI field such as FSL (FMRIB Software Library), PSOM (Pipeline System for Octave and Matlab), Diffusion Toolkit, and MRIcron.

The PANDA toolbox utilizes the original diffusion MRI (dMRI) data provided in DICOM or NIfTI format and streamlines all required processing steps to derive diffusion metrics (such as fractional anisotropy (FA) and mean diffusivity (MD)) that are ready for voxel-wise, atlas-based or Tract-Based Spatial Statistics (TBSS) analysis. The diffusion MR data analyzed by PANDA can be either diffusion gradient directions or diffusion MR signal data. The anatomical brain networks for all subjects can be also obtained using PANDA. Using multiple cores or a distributed computing environment, PANDA enables high throughput processing of large number of diffusion MRI data sets. The required input/output parameters and processing options can be specified using a user-friendly graphical user interface (GUI). The PANDA toolbox is an open source project and available from http://www.nitrc.org/projects/panda/. We believe that PANDA will facilitate and speed up the analysis of diffusion MRI for the research of human structural connectomes.
